content.cn_text: 现在请仔细听，并回答31至40题。
content.en_text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 31 to 40.

content.cn_text: 大家好，我今天的报告是关于大约三万年前史前艺术家创作的洞穴壁画，特别是这些壁画中或附近出现的符号。
content.en_text: Hello everyone, my presentation today is about the cave paintings produced by prehistoric artists about 30,000 years ago and in particular the symbols that appear in or near these paintings.

content.cn_text: 这些符号在世界许多国家的洞穴深处都有发现，但我今天将要讨论的主要是欧洲的符号。
content.en_text: These symbols are found deep in caves in many countries around the world, but the ones I'm going to talk about today are mainly European.

content.cn_text: 首先，简单介绍一下最常见的符号。
content.en_text: Firstly, a little about the most common symbols.

content.cn_text: 已发现的符号形状大小各异。有些符号因其相当简单而易于辨认。
content.en_text: The symbols that have been discovered are of different shapes and sizes. Some are easily recognizable as they're quite simple.

content.cn_text: 最基本的就是一系列的点，但它们也可能包含诸如三角形之类的形状。然而，其他符号则更为复杂。
content.en_text: The most basic are just a series of dots, but they may also include forms such as the triangle. However, other symbols are more complex.

content.cn_text: 一个典型的例子是波浪线。它有时单独使用，但相同的波浪线也作为更大的马的图案的一部分，所以我们认为这就是波浪线所代表的。
content.en_text: A case in point is the wavy line. This is sometimes used on its own, but the same wavy line is also used as part of a larger picture of a horse, so we think this is what the wavy line represents.

content.cn_text: 一个非常重要的符号是所谓的“手形符号”。
content.en_text: One very important symbol is what's called the hand symbol.

content.cn_text: 这经常出现在洞穴的墙壁上。我将更详细地谈谈它。首先，它是如何制作的。颜料本身是由洞穴艺术家可以接触到的各种东西组成的。
content.en_text: This frequently appears on the walls of caves. I'll talk about it in a bit more detail. Firstly, how it was made. The paint itself was made up from a variety of things that cave artists would have had access to.

content.cn_text: 他们有火，所以木炭是一种成分。另一种是他们狩猎动物的脂肪，最后是他们自己口中的唾液。
content.en_text: They had fire, so charcoal was one ingredient. Another was the fat from the animals they would have hunted, and lastly, the saliva from their own mouths.

content.cn_text: 然后是如何将稀薄的颜料涂到洞穴墙壁上的问题。这肯定不容易。他们必须使用某种中空的东西。
content.en_text: Then there's a question of how they actually applied the runny paint onto the walls of the cave. It certainly wouldn't have been easy. They would have had to use something hollow.

content.cn_text: 事实上，它可能看起来像一根吸管，你知道的，就像我们现在用来喝水的。他们会把颜料吸进去，然后吹出来。
content.en_text: In fact, it's likely to have looked like a straw, you know, like the kind we use for drinking nowadays. They'd have sucked the paint up into it, then blown it out.

content.cn_text: 研究人员最近重现了制作手形符号的颜料和过程。艺术家会先将手平放在墙上，然后将颜料吹到墙上，形成手的轮廓。
content.en_text: Researchers have recently recreated both the paint and the processes used to make the hand symbols. The artist would have first put his hand flat against the wall, then blown the paint onto the wall creating an outline of the hand.

content.cn_text: 试图重现这一过程的研究人员表示，这不仅是一项难以掌握的技术，而且还会发出一种奇怪的噪音。
content.en_text: The researchers who've tried to recreate the process have said that not only is it a difficult technique to master, but also that a strange noise is produced.

content.cn_text: 这也让他们感到头晕目眩，所以我们只能想象这些洞穴艺术家也有同样的感觉。
content.en_text: It also led them to feel light-headed, so we can only imagine that these cave artists felt the same.

content.cn_text: 我相信你们都会同意这些符号确实很有趣，但它们也至关重要。研究人员现已确定了法国和西班牙常用的总共26个符号。
content.en_text: I'm sure you'll agree that these symbols are really interesting, but they're also of vital importance. Researchers have now identified a total of 26 symbols commonly used in France and Spain.

content.cn_text: 我们之所以拥有这些知识，是因为一个正在不断更新的数据库。它清楚地表明，许多符号即使在不同地方，也经常以特定的组合使用。
content.en_text: We only have this knowledge thanks to a database that's been created, and which is being added to all the time. It clearly demonstrates that many symbols are often used in particular combinations, even if they're in different places.

content.cn_text: 这很重要，因为看起来当时在该地区生活和旅行的不同洞穴部落都会看到这些标记并加以补充。
content.en_text: This is significant, as it looks like different tribes of cave people who lived and traveled through the area at that time would have seen these markings as well as adding to them.

content.cn_text: 尽管研究人员不认为这些符号本身是一种语言，但他们乐于称之为一种密码。有证据支持这一观点。
content.en_text: Although researchers don't believe that these symbols are a language as such, they are happy to call them a type of code. There is evidence to support this idea.

content.cn_text: 在西班牙的洞穴中，一组由五个符号组成的序列特别常见。在一条用赤鹿牙齿制成的项链上，也发现了完全相同的五个符号序列。
content.en_text: One sequence of five symbols is particularly common in caves in Spain. Exactly the same sequence of five symbols has been found carved into a necklace made from the teeth of a red deer.

content.cn_text: 因此，这种符号组合似乎具有特殊的意义。当然，许多这些解释都是理论而非确凿事实，这也是研究人员对我们所见事物的意义存在分歧的原因。
content.en_text: So it appears that this combination of symbols may have had a special meaning. Of course, many of these explanations are theory as opposed to hard facts, which is why there is disagreement among researchers about the significance of what we're seeing.

content.cn_text: 其中一个理论被称为“大跃进”。这个观点是，大约在35,000年前，创造力突然大增，这导致了包括第一艘船在内的许多发明。然而，并非所有人都同意。
content.en_text: One theory is called the Great Leap Forward. The idea is that around 35,000 years ago there was a sudden increase in creativity which led to the invention of the boat among other things. However, not everyone agrees.

content.cn_text: 事实上，许多科学家认为，在非洲发现的证据似乎表明，这种创造力的增长发生得更早，大约在7万到9万年前，并且是更循序渐进的。
content.en_text: In fact, many scientists believe that what has been found in Africa seems to indicate that this increase in creativity happened much earlier, around 70 to 90,000 years ago and more gradually.

content.cn_text: 一个被用来支持这一观点的文物例子是发现了不同种类的箭，这些箭本来是用于狩猎的。
content.en_text: One example of an artifact that is offered to support the idea is the discovery of different sorts of arrows, which would have been used for hunting.

content.cn_text: 这些科学家认为，之所以没有发现更多早期符号的原因是，它们可能被绘制在没有保存下来的物品上，例如木头。
content.en_text: These scientists suggest that the reason more symbols haven't been found from this earlier time is because they would have been painted on things that did not survive, such as wood.

content.cn_text: 事实上，我们非常幸运，洞穴完好地保护了这些样本，否则我们可能永远无法知道这些艺术家有多么能干。
content.en_text: In fact, we are very lucky that caves have protected these specimens as well as they have, or we might never have known what these artists were capable of.

content.cn_text: 第四部分到此结束。现在您有一分钟的时间检查第四部分的答案。
content.en_text: That is the end of Part 4. You now have one minute to check your answers to Part 4.

content.cn_text: 你
content.en_text: You

content.cn_text: 你
content.en_text: You

content.cn_text: 你
content.en_text: You

groups.desc:
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
groups.title: Questions 31-40.

items.title: Simple shapes, e.g. dots and a 31. _________
More complex shapes, e.g. a wavy line to represent a 32. _________
The paint was made by mixing charcoal with 33. _________ and saliva.
The paint was applied using something similar to a 34. _________
Applying paint made an unusual 35. _________ and also caused lightheadedness.
The symbols are likely to be a 36. _________ used by different groups.
A sequence of five of the symbols has been found on cave walls in Spain and on a 37. _________ made from a deer’s teeth.
Some researchers say the symbols were part of the ‘Great Leap Forward’ which also included innovations such as the first 38. _________
Others say the discovery in Africa of earlier artefacts such as types of 39. _________ disproves the theory.
It is likely that some art painted on 40. _________ did not survive.

groups.source_collect:
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
Ancient cave art symbols
The most common symbols include:
• Simple shapes, e.g. dots and a $31$
• More complex shapes, e.g. a wavy line to represent a $32$
How the hand symbol was made
• The paint was made by mixing charcoal with $33$ and saliva.
• The paint was applied using something similar to a $34$
• Applying paint made an unusual $35$ and also caused lightheadedness.
The importance of the symbols:
• Researchers are developing a database to help identify any patterns.
• The symbols are likely to be a $36$ used by different groups.
• A sequence of five of the symbols has been found on cave walls in Spain and on a $37$ made from a deer’s teeth.
What researchers disagree about
• Some researchers say the symbols were part of the ‘Great Leap Forward’ which also included innovations such as the first $38$
• Others say the discovery in Africa of earlier artefacts such as types of $39$ disproves the theory.
• It is likely that some art painted on $40$ did not survive.
